description: Application of ecological principles and human dimensions to the conservation of biological diversity. Topics: biodiversity science; conservation planning; population ecology; habitat loss, species exploitation, invasive species, pollution; human attitudes and activities as they affect the biosphere; approaches to monitoring interventions.

prerequisites.prerequisites_text: Satisfied Quantitative Reasoning (QR) A requirement andZOOLOGY/​BOTANY  450,F&W ECOL/​BOTANY  455,ZOOLOGY/​BOTANY/​F&W ECOL  460, or graduate/professional standing
